Link: NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT
Title: NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT - Smartcomp 2026
NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
NSF Student Travel Grant for IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 (US-based Students ONLY)
The 12
th
IEEE Conference on Smart Computing (SMARTCOMP 2026), to be held in-person in Messina, Italy, from June 22 to June 25, 2026, is expected to offer NSF-sponsored student travel grants to support participation in SMARTCOMP and its co-located events, including workshops, panels, poster and demo sessions, and a Ph.D. forum.
Overview and Eligibility
Travel grants are intended for full-time students enrolled at colleges and universities in the United States. We welcome applications from students at institutions across the United States.
Acceptance of a paper is not required to be considered for a travel grant. Both student authors and non-author students are eligible to apply. Ph.D. students applying for a travel grant are strongly encouraged to also apply to the Ph.D. forum.
Students must submit an application via the
Google Form link
by April 30, 2026.
Application Requirements
To apply for NSF travel support, please upload the following documents:
Required Document | Details
One-page cover letter | Confirmation that you are a full-time student (B.S., M.S., or Ph.D.)A statement that you will attend the entire SMARTCOMP 2026 conference and co-located eventsA brief summary of your research interests and accomplishments in smart computingA description of how attending SMARTCOMP 2026 would benefit your research and academic goals
Two-page CV | A current two-page curriculum vitae
Letter of recommendation from your academic mentor | Confirmation of your full-time student status and good academic standingConfirmation of your engagement in smart computing research and your potential for contributionsThe benefits you would gain from attending the conferenceYour commitment to attend the entire conference and associated events
Reimbursement Information
Reimbursable expenses include the advance registration fee (for student authors and non-authors), airfare, hotel accommodations, and local transportation
Expenses not eligible for reimbursement include regular or on-site registration, internet fees, and similar incidental costs
Reimbursements will be processed after the conference upon submission of the required receipts
The final reimbursement amount will depend on the total number of awardees and the available funding
Contact
For questions regarding the travel grant program, please contact:
Laura Barnes
Jason O. Hallstrom

Link: REGISTRATION
Title: REGISTRATION - Smartcomp 2026
REGISTRATION - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Registration
Registration for IEEE SmartComp 2026 follows an
all-inclusive model
, providing access to the full conference program. Each registered participant will be entitled to:
Attendance at the main conference sessions
Participation in all workshops and co-located events
Access to coffee breaks and catering services
Admission to social events included in the conference program
Separate registrations for individual workshops are not available.
All attendees, including authors and non-authors, are required to register.
Given an accepted paper for any of the conference tracks (main, industry, workshop, poster/demo paper, or PhD forum), at least one of the authors listed on the paper must attend the conference in person and present the paper according to the schedule provided in advance.
Registration Categories
Participants may register under one of the following categories:
Full Registration (IEEE Member / Non-member)
Student Registration (IEEE Member / Non-member)
Discounted rates are available for IEEE members and students, subject to the conditions below. If you are not already an IEEE member, you may join
here
.
Author Registration Policy
In order for accepted contributions to be included in the conference program and proceedings, the following conditions apply:
Each accepted paper, whether in the main conference, workshops or affiliated events (except PhD Forum), requires a dedicated
full registration
A full registration covers
one paper
A full registration may also cover one additional contribution from satellite tracks (e.g., Demo, Posters, PhD Forum). It does not cover workshop papers.
For authors with multiple accepted papers:
Each additional paper (whether in the main conference or workshops) beyond the first must be registered separately
A
reduced additional paper fee
is available when multiple papers are associated with the same registered author
Student registrations:
Student registrations
cannot be used to cover regular papers
(main conference or workshops)
They are only applicable to student-oriented tracks (e.g., PhD Forum), unless accompanied by a full registration
At least one author per accepted paper must be registered.
Important Dates
(To be confirmed)
Registrations Open:
2nd May 2026
Author registration deadline:
15th May 2026
Early registration deadline:
1st June 2026
Registration Entitlements
A valid registration includes:
Access to all technical sessions and workshops
Access to the conference proceedings
Coffee breaks and scheduled catering services
Participation in social events included in the official program
Payment Policy
Registrations are considered valid only upon receipt of
full payment
Payments must be completed through the official registration system
Any bank charges or transaction fees shall be borne by the participant
Cancellation and Refund Policy
General Refund Policy
Requests submitted within the specified deadline are eligible for a
partial refund
, subject to an administrative fee (€50)
No refunds will be issued after the stated deadline
Visa-related Cases
In the event of visa denial, a refund may be granted upon submission of
official supporting documentation
Author Registrations
Each accepted paper must remain associated with a valid full registration
Failure to comply may result in removal of the paper from the program and proceedings
Additional charges may apply for:
Extra pages exceeding the allowed limit (€100 per page)
Additional tickets for social events (e.g., banquet) (€80)
Registration Fees
Early Registration
Category | Fee
Full – IEEE Member | €820
Full – Non-member | €975
Student – IEEE Member | €550
Student – Non-member | €660
Additional Paper Fee (same author) | €350
Late / On-site Registration
Category | Fee
Full – IEEE Member | €980
Full – Non-member | €1170
Student – IEEE Member | €660
Student – Non-member | €800
Additional Items
Item | Fee
Extra page | €100
Additional banquet ticket | €80
Notes on Additional Papers
The additional paper fee applies only to authors who have already completed a full registration.
Each additional paper must be associated with the same registered author in order to qualify for the reduced fee.
Final Remarks
These policies are aligned with standard IEEE conference practices and comparable international events.
The Organizing Committee reserves the right to make adjustments if necessary.
Participants are strongly encouraged to complete registration within the early deadline to benefit from reduced fees.
REGISTER HERE

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/call-phd-forum/ ===
Title: CALL FOR PHD FORUM - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR PHD FORUM - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for PhD Forum
Call for Contributions: SmartComp 2026 PhD Forum
The Ph.D. Forum at SmartComp 2026 provides an excellent opportunity for Ph.D. students to present their work in progress to the scientific community and obtain individual feedback from leading researchers in the field. This forum is intended to provide doctoral students with a platform to interact with established researchers from both academia and industry and get useful guidance on the broader impacts of their research.
PhD Forum Format
The Forum will be organized as a workshop co-located with SmartComp. In addition, the Forum will be supplemented by a one-minute-madness session plus a poster session during the main conference, to facilitate extended interaction opportunities between Ph.D. student participants and researchers from academia, industry, and government.
During the workshop, senior members of the community will be present to provide constructive technical comments to each attendee. The Forum will provide a working environment for students to refine and practice their presentations, helping them improve communication skills, and to collaboratively develop research ideas for the PhD research and beyond. Special recognition will be given to the most innovative and/or most promising research project(s) presented at the Ph.D. Forum based on the pitch talk, the presented poster, and the discussion with the participants during the poster session.
Current Ph.D. students at any stage of their career are encouraged to submit a 3-page extended abstract about their research. Submissions are expected to include motivation of the importance and challenges to the particular problem, and initial results that support either the importance of the research problem or viability of solutions.
The abstract should include the author’s name, affiliation and email address. The name of the supervisor should be marked (“supervised by …”) on the paper, under the author’s name. Authors of accepted submissions are expected to participate in-person. The proceedings of the Forum will be included in the proceedings of the main conference.
Submission Guidelines
Submissions must be in PDF format, be no more than three pages in length (all included) and adhere to the IEEE two-column conference format (the same format used for paper submissions to SmartComp2026).
All manuscripts must be registered and submitted through the
EDAS Submission Site.
Participation
Authors of accepted submissions are expected to participate in the Ph.D. Forum (a half-day workshop immediately prior to the main conference), and to deliver an elevator pitch and A1 poster presentation for their work during the main program of the SmartComp2026 conference.
Participants who are current full-time students are eligible for the student registration fee. However, please note that any other publication in SmartComp—including main conference papers, posters, demos, workshop papers, or work-in-progress submissions—requires at least one author to register at the full rate. If a student planning to attend the PhD Forum is also a co-author of any such publication, and no other co-author is registering at the full rate, the student must register at the full rate and is not eligible for the student rate.
Some students will also be able to apply for travel grants (when details are available, they will be published on the SmartComp website).
Important Dates
Submission:
15th April 2026
24th April 2026
Acceptance notification:
20th April 2026
3rd May 2026
Camera ready:
30th April 2026
6th May 2026
SMARTCOMP 2026 PhD Forum Chairs
Franca Delmastro
, IIT-CNR, Italy
Simone Silvestri
, University of Kentucky, USA

Title: IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
Skip to content
IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
Messina, Italy - Department of Engineering
22 - 25 June 2026
PROGRAMME
REGISTRATION
IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain enabled by advances in sensor-based technologies, Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems, edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, ambient intelligence, and artificial intelligence.
Applications of smart computing can be found in diverse societal domains, including transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, industrial systems, precision farming, and entertainment. Algorithmic and systems advancements in edge, fog, or cloud computing, mobile and pervasive computing, sensor networking, and social computing are accelerating such innovation while expanding its influence in everyday ways of living.
SMARTCOMP 2026 welcomes novel, cutting-edge and creative research contributions. Authors should discuss the importance, timeliness, and significance of their work as well as limitations, implications, future opportunities and open research directions.

Link: CALLS
Title: CALL FOR PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Research Papers
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain enabled by advances in sensor-based technologies, Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems, edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, ambient intelligence, and artificial intelligence.
Applications of smart computing can be found in diverse societal domains, including transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, industrial systems, precision farming, and entertainment. Algorithmic and systems advancements in edge, fog, or cloud computing, mobile and pervasive computing, sensor networking, and social computing are accelerating such innovation while expanding its influence in everyday ways of living.
SMARTCOMP 2026 welcomes novel, cutting-edge and creative research contributions. Authors should discuss the importance, timeliness, and significance of their work as well as limitations, implications, future opportunities and open research directions.
Main Research Track
SMARTCOMP 2026 solicits papers that address the core challenges of smart computing, focusing on the design and development of scalable, dependable systems, and on leveraging computing technologies to enhance resource sustainability and enhance sustainable quality of human life.
Topics of interest include – but are not limited– to the following:
Emerging Smart Computing Paradigms
Algorithms for Smart Computing
AI and Machine Learning in Smart Computing
Mobile and Ubiquitous Smart Systems, Wearable Computing and IoT
Intelligent Cyber-Physical Systems
Cloud, Edge, and Fog based Smart Computing Platforms
Data Engineering and Analytics for Smart Computing
Distributed Sensing, AI, and Computation
Middleware for Smart Systems
Novel Communication Paradigms in Smart Computing
Low Power Communication and Computing for Smart Systems
Social Computing and Smart Systems
Quantum Computing in Smart Systems
Security, Privacy, and Trust in Smart Computing
Fairness and Socio-technical Aspects of Smart Computing
Interdisciplinary Approaches to Smart Computing
Novel applications of Smart Computing in emerging and high potential fields (e.g. Smart Communities, Health, Industry, Transport, Energy, Space, Agrifood, …)
Important Dates
Paper Submission:
1st February 2026
16th February (AoE)
Acceptance Notification:
15th April 2026
Camera Ready:
8th May 2026
Conference:
22 to 25 June 2026
All deadlines are Anywhere on Earth.
Submission Guidelines
Paper submissions must be no longer than 8 pages including references and formatted using the two-column IEEE proceedings template. All submitted papers following these requirements will be peer reviewed by the Technical Program Committee. All presented papers will be published in the conference proceedings and submitted to the IEEEXplore Digital Library.
The conference will be in-person and no virtual or remote presentations will be permitted.
Accepted papers must be presented in person by one of the authors under full registration. Failure to meet this requirement will result in the exclusion of the paper from the final proceedings, the conference program, and the IEEE Digital Library.
Authors of accepted papers are responsible to obtain an Italian visa, if required, in a timely manner so as to meet the above expectations.
The conference applies a single-blind review process.
Submit your paper here
.
Paper registration opens on November 1st, 2025
One additional page for the camera-ready version can be purchased after the paper review and selection.
BEST PAPER AWARD and Editorial Follow-Ups
The authors of the best paper will receive an award in recognition to their contribution to the field.
Extended versions of selected high-quality papers accepted to the main conference will be considered for publication in
Elsevier’s Pervasive and Mobile Computing journal (IF: 3.5)
Technical Program Committee Co-Chairs
Daniela Nicklas
George Roussos
Contact Information
For any questions about the conference, please contact the TPC Co-Chairs.

Title: CALL FOR INDUSTRY PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR INDUSTRY PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Industry Track Papers
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
In addition to the main research track, the conference invites innovative applications and advances in the industrial use of smart computing technology.
Industry Track
The industry track will provide a forum to discuss novel ideas and emerging results, present innovative applications and tools, and bring about novel research questions, approaches, and directions with an industry focus. The conference industry track draws upon many industrial applications such as transportation, energy, environmental protection, resource management, healthcare, security, banking, entertainment, and social media.
An important criterion for papers to be submitted to the Industry Track is the clear industrial association, either in the form of an extensive evaluation of research results in industrial use cases or the active participation of industry representatives as co-authors.
Accepted papers will be published as part of a separate section of the main conference proceedings that highlights the Industry relevance of the submission.
Industry Track Topics include, but are not limited to, the following:
Smart Cyber-Physical Systems
for Energy, Water, Agriculture, Transportation, Healthcare, Banking Infrastructure, and Smart Communities
Security, Privacy, and Economics
of Smart Environments
Human-Centric Smart Environments
for Health, Entertainment, and Social Interaction
Artificial Intelligence

for Smart Computing Systems
Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT)
and
Intelligent Edge Systems
Smart and Connected Communities across Cities
Emerging Smart Computing Paradigms

for Smart Systems

in Smart Systems

Link: CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS
Title: CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Workshop Proposals
The organising committee of the IEEE International Conference on Smart Computing (SMARTCOMP) 2026 invites proposals for workshops to be held in conjunction with the main conference in Messina, Italy, on June 22
nd
, 2026. IEEE SMARTCOMP is the premier forum for exploring cutting-edge research, innovation, and applications in the rapidly evolving domain of smart computing, encompassing advancements in sensor-based technologies, IoT, cyber-physical systems, edge and cloud computing, big data, machine learning, and AI.
The workshops at IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 aim to encourage researchers, practitioners, and industry experts to exchange ideas, discuss emerging trends, and explore novel approaches within the field of smart computing.
The Workshops co-chairs of IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 invite proposals for half or full day (22
nd
June 2026) workshops affiliated with the conference. Workshops should provide opportunities for deep dives into focused areas, promote lively interactions, and stimulate collaborative research in both emerging and established topics.
Submission Guidelines
Each workshop proposal must include the following:
Workshop Title and Acronym, where applicable.
Organisers (Workshops may have
up to three organisers
. It is strongly recommended that organisers belong to at least two different institutions):
Names
Affiliations
Short bios of up to 200 words for each organizer
A brief description (up to 2 pages) including:
Research and technological issues to be covered in the workshop;
Justification for organising the workshop in SMARTCOMP 2026;
A short discussion on the distinguishing features of this workshop compared to other available workshops and conferences;
A description of the research groups the workshop aims to attract;
Names of potential Program Committee members;
A tentative format of the workshop (e.g., keynotes, paper presentations) and duration (e.g., one-day, half-day), and a strategy to facilitate lively discussions and involvement of the attendees;
If applicable, a description of past versions of the workshop, including dates, organisers, number of submitted and accepted papers, and number of attendees;
An estimate of the expected number of submissions and likely attendees if this is the first time such a workshop proposal is submitted to IEEE SMARTCOMP.
A tentative Call for Papers (as complete as possible);
A description of the plans for publicity and workshop Website.
Submission Method
Workshop proposals should be submitted in PDF format by email with the subject line “IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 Workshop Proposal” to the Workshop Co-Chairs: Dirk Pesch (dirk.pesch@ucc.ie) and Francesca Righetti (francesca.righetti@unipi.it), no later than Wednesday, 10 December 2025. More details on IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 can be found at our home page
here
.

Notification of Acceptance
: 17th December 2025

9th March 2026
23rd March 2026 (FIRM)

, 22
nd
June 2026
Publication
Each accepted paper will require a full SMARTCOMP registration (workshops only registrations will not be available) and in-person presentation. All accepted papers presented in the workshop will be published in the proceedings of the conference and published in the IEEE Xplore Digital Library and Scopus indexed. Papers that are not presented at the workshop will not be published in the proceedings.

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/call-for-posters/ ===
Link: CALL FOR POSTERS/DEMOS
Title: CALL FOR POSTERS/DEMOS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR POSTERS/DEMOS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Posters/Demos/WIP
SMARTCOMP 2026 is the 12th edition of the premier conference on Smart Computing. Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain based on the synergistic influence of advances in sensor-based technologies, the Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems (CPS), edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, and artificial intelligence. Applications of smart computing can be found in different societal domains including, but not limited to, transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, banking, industrial systems, entertainment, and social media. Algorithmic and system advancements in cloud computing, mobile/pervasive computing, cyber-physical systems, sensor networking, and social computing are taking smart computing to a new dimension and improving our ways of living.
The Posters and Work in Progress (WIP) session will provide a forum to discuss novel ideas, preliminary results, present innovative applications and tools, and bring about novel research questions, approaches, and directions.
The Demo session will focus on hands-on demonstrations of preliminary works on novel platforms, implementations, and case studies. We particularly welcome demos that present innovative designs or advancements in CPS, providing attendees with the opportunity to experience cutting-edge technology firsthand.
We encourage both academia and industry to contribute by sharing valuable insights into real-world applications and innovative solutions in Smart Computing. Industry submissions are invited to reflect practical experiences, field trials, and emerging trends from an industrial perspective, demonstrating the impact and applicability of Smart Computing technologies.
Authors of papers that received favorable reviews but could not be accepted to the Main track may be offered the opportunity to publish their submitted work in the Posters or Demo tracks.
Topics
Areas of interest include (but are not limited to):
Future Smart Computing Paradigms
Models of Smart Environments
Algorithms for Smart Computing
AI and Machine Learning in Smart Computing
Security, Privacy, and Trust Issues in Smart Computing
Fairness and Socio-technical Issues of Smart Computing
Cyber-physical System Platforms for Smart Environments
Middleware Platforms for Smart Environments
Mobile and Ubiquitous Platforms for Smart Environments
Cloud, Edge, and Fog Computing Platforms for Smart Systems
Data Architectures and Analytics for Smart Computing
Smart Computing for Smart and Connected Communities
Smart Green Computing
Social Computing and Smart Systems
Wearable Computing and IoT for Smart Systems
Architectures and Software for Smart Computing
Novel Communication Paradigms (e.g., 5G/6G, VLC, DSA) in Smart Computing
Quantum Computing in Smart Systems
Interdisciplinary Approaches to Smart Computing
Applications of Smart Computing include:
Smart healthcare and digital epidemiology
Smart agriculture
Smart infrastructures
Smart cities
Smart energy, transportation, water distribution systems
Smart factories
Smart workspace
Important Dates
Paper Submission:
22nd March 2026
17th April 2026 (AoE)
Acceptance Notification:
1st May 2026
Camera Ready:
8th May 2026
Conference:
22 to 25 June 2026
All deadlines are Anywhere on Earth.
Submission Guidelines
Posters, Demos, and WIP papers should be no more than 3 pages in length, including figures, tables, and references.
All the submissions must be formatted according to the two-column IEEE proceedings template. IEEE provides corresponding formatting templates at
IEEE proceedings template
. Make sure to use the conference mode of the template, i.e., LaTeX users must use the conference option of the IEEEtran document class. While the submission format is the same,
the goals for Posters/WIP and Demos are different
:
Posters/WIP
: We welcome papers up to 3 pages discussing early or ongoing research. Posters/WIP are intended to present ongoing research with a focus on novel methodologies, preliminary results, or innovative concepts that hold promise for future development, highlighting key ideas, or research directions in a concise format, fostering discussion and feedback on emerging work/applications.
Demos
: We welcome papers up to 3 pages focused on the specific component that will be demonstrated to the audience and how the attendees will be able to interact. They should also provide an overview of the background research leading up to the demo.
All submitted Posters and Demos will be subject to peer reviews by SMARTCOMP Technical Program Committee members and other experts in the field.
At least one author of each accepted paper must register
and attend the conference to present the work.
Papers must be submitted electronically as a single PDF file on US Letter size paper (not A4), with all fonts embedded (the PDF-A standard complies with that), through EDAS.
Submit your Demo here.
Submit your Poster/WIP here.
*** Accepted Posters, Demos, and WIP papers will be published on IEEE Xplore! ***
Posters/Demos/WIP Co-Chairs
Brent Lagesse
Mario Molinara

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/nfs-student-travel-grant/ ===
Link: NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT
Title: NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT - Smartcomp 2026
NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
NSF Student Travel Grant for IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 (US-based Students ONLY)
The 12
th
IEEE Conference on Smart Computing (SMARTCOMP 2026), to be held in-person in Messina, Italy, from June 22 to June 25, 2026, is expected to offer NSF-sponsored student travel grants to support participation in SMARTCOMP and its co-located events, including workshops, panels, poster and demo sessions, and a Ph.D. forum.
Overview and Eligibility
Travel grants are intended for full-time students enrolled at colleges and universities in the United States. We welcome applications from students at institutions across the United States.
Acceptance of a paper is not required to be considered for a travel grant. Both student authors and non-author students are eligible to apply. Ph.D. students applying for a travel grant are strongly encouraged to also apply to the Ph.D. forum.
Students must submit an application via the
Google Form link
by April 30, 2026.
Application Requirements
To apply for NSF travel support, please upload the following documents:
Required Document | Details
One-page cover letter | Confirmation that you are a full-time student (B.S., M.S., or Ph.D.)A statement that you will attend the entire SMARTCOMP 2026 conference and co-located eventsA brief summary of your research interests and accomplishments in smart computingA description of how attending SMARTCOMP 2026 would benefit your research and academic goals
Two-page CV | A current two-page curriculum vitae
Letter of recommendation from your academic mentor | Confirmation of your full-time student status and good academic standingConfirmation of your engagement in smart computing research and your potential for contributionsThe benefits you would gain from attending the conferenceYour commitment to attend the entire conference and associated events
Reimbursement Information
Reimbursable expenses include the advance registration fee (for student authors and non-authors), airfare, hotel accommodations, and local transportation
Expenses not eligible for reimbursement include regular or on-site registration, internet fees, and similar incidental costs
Reimbursements will be processed after the conference upon submission of the required receipts
The final reimbursement amount will depend on the total number of awardees and the available funding
Contact
For questions regarding the travel grant program, please contact:
Laura Barnes
Jason O. Hallstrom

Link: REGISTRATION
Title: REGISTRATION - Smartcomp 2026
REGISTRATION - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Registration
Registration for IEEE SmartComp 2026 follows an
all-inclusive model
, providing access to the full conference program. Each registered participant will be entitled to:
Attendance at the main conference sessions
Participation in all workshops and co-located events
Access to coffee breaks and catering services
Admission to social events included in the conference program
Separate registrations for individual workshops are not available.
All attendees, including authors and non-authors, are required to register.
Given an accepted paper for any of the conference tracks (main, industry, workshop, poster/demo paper, or PhD forum), at least one of the authors listed on the paper must attend the conference in person and present the paper according to the schedule provided in advance.
Registration Categories
Participants may register under one of the following categories:
Full Registration (IEEE Member / Non-member)
Student Registration (IEEE Member / Non-member)
Discounted rates are available for IEEE members and students, subject to the conditions below. If you are not already an IEEE member, you may join
here
.
Author Registration Policy
In order for accepted contributions to be included in the conference program and proceedings, the following conditions apply:
Each accepted paper, whether in the main conference, workshops or affiliated events (except PhD Forum), requires a dedicated
full registration
A full registration covers
one paper
A full registration may also cover one additional contribution from satellite tracks (e.g., Demo, Posters, PhD Forum). It does not cover workshop papers.
For authors with multiple accepted papers:
Each additional paper (whether in the main conference or workshops) beyond the first must be registered separately
A
reduced additional paper fee
is available when multiple papers are associated with the same registered author
Student registrations:
Student registrations
cannot be used to cover regular papers
(main conference or workshops)
They are only applicable to student-oriented tracks (e.g., PhD Forum), unless accompanied by a full registration
At least one author per accepted paper must be registered.
Important Dates
(To be confirmed)

2nd May 2026
Author registration deadline:
15th May 2026
Early registration deadline:
1st June 2026
Registration Entitlements
A valid registration includes:
Access to all technical sessions and workshops
Access to the conference proceedings
Coffee breaks and scheduled catering services
Participation in social events included in the official program
Payment Policy
Registrations are considered valid only upon receipt of
full payment
Payments must be completed through the official registration system
Any bank charges or transaction fees shall be borne by the participant
Cancellation and Refund Policy
General Refund Policy
Requests submitted within the specified deadline are eligible for a
partial refund
, subject to an administrative fee (€50)
No refunds will be issued after the stated deadline
Visa-related Cases
In the event of visa denial, a refund may be granted upon submission of
official supporting documentation
Author Registrations
Each accepted paper must remain associated with a valid full registration
Failure to comply may result in removal of the paper from the program and proceedings
Additional charges may apply for:
Extra pages exceeding the allowed limit (€100 per page)
Additional tickets for social events (e.g., banquet) (€80)

Final Remarks
These policies are aligned with standard IEEE conference practices and comparable international events.
The Organizing Committee reserves the right to make adjustments if necessary.

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/call-phd-forum/ ===
Title: CALL FOR PHD FORUM - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR PHD FORUM - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for PhD Forum
Call for Contributions: SmartComp 2026 PhD Forum
The Ph.D. Forum at SmartComp 2026 provides an excellent opportunity for Ph.D. students to present their work in progress to the scientific community and obtain individual feedback from leading researchers in the field. This forum is intended to provide doctoral students with a platform to interact with established researchers from both academia and industry and get useful guidance on the broader impacts of their research.
PhD Forum Format
The Forum will be organized as a workshop co-located with SmartComp. In addition, the Forum will be supplemented by a one-minute-madness session plus a poster session during the main conference, to facilitate extended interaction opportunities between Ph.D. student participants and researchers from academia, industry, and government.
During the workshop, senior members of the community will be present to provide constructive technical comments to each attendee. The Forum will provide a working environment for students to refine and practice their presentations, helping them improve communication skills, and to collaboratively develop research ideas for the PhD research and beyond. Special recognition will be given to the most innovative and/or most promising research project(s) presented at the Ph.D. Forum based on the pitch talk, the presented poster, and the discussion with the participants during the poster session.
Current Ph.D. students at any stage of their career are encouraged to submit a 3-page extended abstract about their research. Submissions are expected to include motivation of the importance and challenges to the particular problem, and initial results that support either the importance of the research problem or viability of solutions.
The abstract should include the author’s name, affiliation and email address. The name of the supervisor should be marked (“supervised by …”) on the paper, under the author’s name. Authors of accepted submissions are expected to participate in-person. The proceedings of the Forum will be included in the proceedings of the main conference.
Submission Guidelines
Submissions must be in PDF format, be no more than three pages in length (all included) and adhere to the IEEE two-column conference format (the same format used for paper submissions to SmartComp2026).
All manuscripts must be registered and submitted through the
EDAS Submission Site.
Participation
Authors of accepted submissions are expected to participate in the Ph.D. Forum (a half-day workshop immediately prior to the main conference), and to deliver an elevator pitch and A1 poster presentation for their work during the main program of the SmartComp2026 conference.
Participants who are current full-time students are eligible for the student registration fee. However, please note that any other publication in SmartComp—including main conference papers, posters, demos, workshop papers, or work-in-progress submissions—requires at least one author to register at the full rate. If a student planning to attend the PhD Forum is also a co-author of any such publication, and no other co-author is registering at the full rate, the student must register at the full rate and is not eligible for the student rate.
Some students will also be able to apply for travel grants (when details are available, they will be published on the SmartComp website).
Important Dates
Submission:
15th April 2026
24th April 2026
Acceptance notification:
20th April 2026
3rd May 2026
Camera ready:
30th April 2026
6th May 2026
SMARTCOMP 2026 PhD Forum Chairs
Franca Delmastro
, IIT-CNR, Italy
Simone Silvestri
, University of Kentucky, USA

Title: IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
Skip to content
IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
Messina, Italy - Department of Engineering
22 - 25 June 2026
PROGRAMME
REGISTRATION
IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain enabled by advances in sensor-based technologies, Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems, edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, ambient intelligence, and artificial intelligence.
Applications of smart computing can be found in diverse societal domains, including transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, industrial systems, precision farming, and entertainment. Algorithmic and systems advancements in edge, fog, or cloud computing, mobile and pervasive computing, sensor networking, and social computing are accelerating such innovation while expanding its influence in everyday ways of living.
SMARTCOMP 2026 welcomes novel, cutting-edge and creative research contributions. Authors should discuss the importance, timeliness, and significance of their work as well as limitations, implications, future opportunities and open research directions.

Link: CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS
Title: CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Workshop Proposals
The organising committee of the IEEE International Conference on Smart Computing (SMARTCOMP) 2026 invites proposals for workshops to be held in conjunction with the main conference in Messina, Italy, on June 22
nd
, 2026. IEEE SMARTCOMP is the premier forum for exploring cutting-edge research, innovation, and applications in the rapidly evolving domain of smart computing, encompassing advancements in sensor-based technologies, IoT, cyber-physical systems, edge and cloud computing, big data, machine learning, and AI.
The workshops at IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 aim to encourage researchers, practitioners, and industry experts to exchange ideas, discuss emerging trends, and explore novel approaches within the field of smart computing.
The Workshops co-chairs of IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 invite proposals for half or full day (22
nd
June 2026) workshops affiliated with the conference. Workshops should provide opportunities for deep dives into focused areas, promote lively interactions, and stimulate collaborative research in both emerging and established topics.
Submission Guidelines
Each workshop proposal must include the following:
Workshop Title and Acronym, where applicable.
Organisers (Workshops may have
up to three organisers
. It is strongly recommended that organisers belong to at least two different institutions):
Names
Affiliations
Short bios of up to 200 words for each organizer
A brief description (up to 2 pages) including:
Research and technological issues to be covered in the workshop;
Justification for organising the workshop in SMARTCOMP 2026;
A short discussion on the distinguishing features of this workshop compared to other available workshops and conferences;
A description of the research groups the workshop aims to attract;
Names of potential Program Committee members;
A tentative format of the workshop (e.g., keynotes, paper presentations) and duration (e.g., one-day, half-day), and a strategy to facilitate lively discussions and involvement of the attendees;
If applicable, a description of past versions of the workshop, including dates, organisers, number of submitted and accepted papers, and number of attendees;
An estimate of the expected number of submissions and likely attendees if this is the first time such a workshop proposal is submitted to IEEE SMARTCOMP.
A tentative Call for Papers (as complete as possible);
A description of the plans for publicity and workshop Website.
Submission Method
Workshop proposals should be submitted in PDF format by email with the subject line “IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 Workshop Proposal” to the Workshop Co-Chairs: Dirk Pesch (dirk.pesch@ucc.ie) and Francesca Righetti (francesca.righetti@unipi.it), no later than Wednesday, 10 December 2025. More details on IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 can be found at our home page
here
.
Important Dates
Workshop Proposal Submission Deadline
: 10th December 2025
Notification of Acceptance
: 17th December 2025
Workshop Papers Submission Deadline
:
9th March 2026
23rd March 2026 (FIRM)
Workshop Date: Monday
, 22
nd
June 2026
Publication
Each accepted paper will require a full SMARTCOMP registration (workshops only registrations will not be available) and in-person presentation. All accepted papers presented in the workshop will be published in the proceedings of the conference and published in the IEEE Xplore Digital Library and Scopus indexed. Papers that are not presented at the workshop will not be published in the proceedings.

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/call-for-posters/ ===
Link: CALL FOR POSTERS/DEMOS
Title: CALL FOR POSTERS/DEMOS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR POSTERS/DEMOS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Posters/Demos/WIP
SMARTCOMP 2026 is the 12th edition of the premier conference on Smart Computing. Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain based on the synergistic influence of advances in sensor-based technologies, the Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems (CPS), edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, and artificial intelligence. Applications of smart computing can be found in different societal domains including, but not limited to, transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, banking, industrial systems, entertainment, and social media. Algorithmic and system advancements in cloud computing, mobile/pervasive computing, cyber-physical systems, sensor networking, and social computing are taking smart computing to a new dimension and improving our ways of living.
The Posters and Work in Progress (WIP) session will provide a forum to discuss novel ideas, preliminary results, present innovative applications and tools, and bring about novel research questions, approaches, and directions.
The Demo session will focus on hands-on demonstrations of preliminary works on novel platforms, implementations, and case studies. We particularly welcome demos that present innovative designs or advancements in CPS, providing attendees with the opportunity to experience cutting-edge technology firsthand.
We encourage both academia and industry to contribute by sharing valuable insights into real-world applications and innovative solutions in Smart Computing. Industry submissions are invited to reflect practical experiences, field trials, and emerging trends from an industrial perspective, demonstrating the impact and applicability of Smart Computing technologies.
Authors of papers that received favorable reviews but could not be accepted to the Main track may be offered the opportunity to publish their submitted work in the Posters or Demo tracks.
Topics
Areas of interest include (but are not limited to):
Future Smart Computing Paradigms
Models of Smart Environments
Algorithms for Smart Computing
AI and Machine Learning in Smart Computing
Security, Privacy, and Trust Issues in Smart Computing
Fairness and Socio-technical Issues of Smart Computing
Cyber-physical System Platforms for Smart Environments
Middleware Platforms for Smart Environments
Mobile and Ubiquitous Platforms for Smart Environments
Cloud, Edge, and Fog Computing Platforms for Smart Systems
Data Architectures and Analytics for Smart Computing
Smart Computing for Smart and Connected Communities
Smart Green Computing
Social Computing and Smart Systems
Wearable Computing and IoT for Smart Systems
Architectures and Software for Smart Computing
Novel Communication Paradigms (e.g., 5G/6G, VLC, DSA) in Smart Computing
Quantum Computing in Smart Systems
Interdisciplinary Approaches to Smart Computing
Applications of Smart Computing include:
Smart healthcare and digital epidemiology
Smart agriculture
Smart infrastructures
Smart cities
Smart energy, transportation, water distribution systems
Smart factories
Smart workspace
Important Dates
Paper Submission:
22nd March 2026
17th April 2026 (AoE)
Acceptance Notification:
1st May 2026
Camera Ready:
8th May 2026
Conference:
22 to 25 June 2026
All deadlines are Anywhere on Earth.
Submission Guidelines
Posters, Demos, and WIP papers should be no more than 3 pages in length, including figures, tables, and references.
All the submissions must be formatted according to the two-column IEEE proceedings template. IEEE provides corresponding formatting templates at
IEEE proceedings template
. Make sure to use the conference mode of the template, i.e., LaTeX users must use the conference option of the IEEEtran document class. While the submission format is the same,
the goals for Posters/WIP and Demos are different
:
Posters/WIP
: We welcome papers up to 3 pages discussing early or ongoing research. Posters/WIP are intended to present ongoing research with a focus on novel methodologies, preliminary results, or innovative concepts that hold promise for future development, highlighting key ideas, or research directions in a concise format, fostering discussion and feedback on emerging work/applications.
Demos
: We welcome papers up to 3 pages focused on the specific component that will be demonstrated to the audience and how the attendees will be able to interact. They should also provide an overview of the background research leading up to the demo.
All submitted Posters and Demos will be subject to peer reviews by SMARTCOMP Technical Program Committee members and other experts in the field.
At least one author of each accepted paper must register
and attend the conference to present the work.
Papers must be submitted electronically as a single PDF file on US Letter size paper (not A4), with all fonts embedded (the PDF-A standard complies with that), through EDAS.
Submit your Demo here.
Submit your Poster/WIP here.
*** Accepted Posters, Demos, and WIP papers will be published on IEEE Xplore! ***
Posters/Demos/WIP Co-Chairs
Brent Lagesse
Mario Molinara

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/nfs-student-travel-grant/ ===
Link: NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT
Title: NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT - Smartcomp 2026
NFS STUDENT TRAVEL GRANT - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
NSF Student Travel Grant for IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 (US-based Students ONLY)
The 12
th
IEEE Conference on Smart Computing (SMARTCOMP 2026), to be held in-person in Messina, Italy, from June 22 to June 25, 2026, is expected to offer NSF-sponsored student travel grants to support participation in SMARTCOMP and its co-located events, including workshops, panels, poster and demo sessions, and a Ph.D. forum.
Overview and Eligibility
Travel grants are intended for full-time students enrolled at colleges and universities in the United States. We welcome applications from students at institutions across the United States.
Acceptance of a paper is not required to be considered for a travel grant. Both student authors and non-author students are eligible to apply. Ph.D. students applying for a travel grant are strongly encouraged to also apply to the Ph.D. forum.
Students must submit an application via the
Google Form link
by April 30, 2026.
Application Requirements
To apply for NSF travel support, please upload the following documents:
Required Document | Details
One-page cover letter | Confirmation that you are a full-time student (B.S., M.S., or Ph.D.)A statement that you will attend the entire SMARTCOMP 2026 conference and co-located eventsA brief summary of your research interests and accomplishments in smart computingA description of how attending SMARTCOMP 2026 would benefit your research and academic goals
Two-page CV | A current two-page curriculum vitae
Letter of recommendation from your academic mentor | Confirmation of your full-time student status and good academic standingConfirmation of your engagement in smart computing research and your potential for contributionsThe benefits you would gain from attending the conferenceYour commitment to attend the entire conference and associated events
Reimbursement Information
Reimbursable expenses include the advance registration fee (for student authors and non-authors), airfare, hotel accommodations, and local transportation
Expenses not eligible for reimbursement include regular or on-site registration, internet fees, and similar incidental costs
Reimbursements will be processed after the conference upon submission of the required receipts
The final reimbursement amount will depend on the total number of awardees and the available funding
Contact
For questions regarding the travel grant program, please contact:
Laura Barnes
Jason O. Hallstrom

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/cfp/ ===
Link: CALLS
Title: CALL FOR PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Research Papers
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain enabled by advances in sensor-based technologies, Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems, edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, ambient intelligence, and artificial intelligence.
Applications of smart computing can be found in diverse societal domains, including transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, industrial systems, precision farming, and entertainment. Algorithmic and systems advancements in edge, fog, or cloud computing, mobile and pervasive computing, sensor networking, and social computing are accelerating such innovation while expanding its influence in everyday ways of living.
SMARTCOMP 2026 welcomes novel, cutting-edge and creative research contributions. Authors should discuss the importance, timeliness, and significance of their work as well as limitations, implications, future opportunities and open research directions.
Main Research Track
SMARTCOMP 2026 solicits papers that address the core challenges of smart computing, focusing on the design and development of scalable, dependable systems, and on leveraging computing technologies to enhance resource sustainability and enhance sustainable quality of human life.
Topics of interest include – but are not limited– to the following:
Emerging Smart Computing Paradigms
Algorithms for Smart Computing
AI and Machine Learning in Smart Computing
Mobile and Ubiquitous Smart Systems, Wearable Computing and IoT
Intelligent Cyber-Physical Systems
Cloud, Edge, and Fog based Smart Computing Platforms
Data Engineering and Analytics for Smart Computing
Distributed Sensing, AI, and Computation
Middleware for Smart Systems
Novel Communication Paradigms in Smart Computing
Low Power Communication and Computing for Smart Systems
Social Computing and Smart Systems
Quantum Computing in Smart Systems
Security, Privacy, and Trust in Smart Computing
Fairness and Socio-technical Aspects of Smart Computing
Interdisciplinary Approaches to Smart Computing
Novel applications of Smart Computing in emerging and high potential fields (e.g. Smart Communities, Health, Industry, Transport, Energy, Space, Agrifood, …)
Important Dates
Paper Submission:
1st February 2026
16th February (AoE)
Acceptance Notification:
15th April 2026
Camera Ready:
8th May 2026
Conference:
22 to 25 June 2026
All deadlines are Anywhere on Earth.
Submission Guidelines
Paper submissions must be no longer than 8 pages including references and formatted using the two-column IEEE proceedings template. All submitted papers following these requirements will be peer reviewed by the Technical Program Committee. All presented papers will be published in the conference proceedings and submitted to the IEEEXplore Digital Library.
The conference will be in-person and no virtual or remote presentations will be permitted.
Accepted papers must be presented in person by one of the authors under full registration. Failure to meet this requirement will result in the exclusion of the paper from the final proceedings, the conference program, and the IEEE Digital Library.
Authors of accepted papers are responsible to obtain an Italian visa, if required, in a timely manner so as to meet the above expectations.
The conference applies a single-blind review process.
Submit your paper here
.
Paper registration opens on November 1st, 2025
One additional page for the camera-ready version can be purchased after the paper review and selection.
BEST PAPER AWARD and Editorial Follow-Ups
The authors of the best paper will receive an award in recognition to their contribution to the field.
Extended versions of selected high-quality papers accepted to the main conference will be considered for publication in
Elsevier’s Pervasive and Mobile Computing journal (IF: 3.5)
Technical Program Committee Co-Chairs
Daniela Nicklas
George Roussos
Contact Information
For any questions about the conference, please contact the TPC Co-Chairs.
Manage consent

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/cfp-industry/ ===
Link: CALL FOR INDUSTRY PAPERS
Title: CALL FOR INDUSTRY PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR INDUSTRY PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Industry Track Papers
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
In addition to the main research track, the conference invites innovative applications and advances in the industrial use of smart computing technology.
Industry Track
The industry track will provide a forum to discuss novel ideas and emerging results, present innovative applications and tools, and bring about novel research questions, approaches, and directions with an industry focus. The conference industry track draws upon many industrial applications such as transportation, energy, environmental protection, resource management, healthcare, security, banking, entertainment, and social media.
An important criterion for papers to be submitted to the Industry Track is the clear industrial association, either in the form of an extensive evaluation of research results in industrial use cases or the active participation of industry representatives as co-authors.
Accepted papers will be published as part of a separate section of the main conference proceedings that highlights the Industry relevance of the submission.
Industry Track Topics include, but are not limited to, the following:
Smart Cyber-Physical Systems
for Energy, Water, Agriculture, Transportation, Healthcare, Banking Infrastructure, and Smart Communities
Security, Privacy, and Economics
of Smart Environments
Human-Centric Smart Environments
for Health, Entertainment, and Social Interaction
Artificial Intelligence

Machine Learning
for Smart Computing Systems
Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT)

for Smart Systems

in Smart Systems

. All submitted papers following these requirements will be peer reviewed by the Technical Program Committee.
All presented papers will be published in the conference proceedings and submitted to the IEEEXplore Digital Library.
The conference will be in-person and
no virtual or remote presentations will be permitted.
Accepted papers must be presented in person by one of the authors under full registration. Failure to meet this requirement will result in the exclusion of the paper from the final proceedings, the conference program, and the IEEE Digital Library.
Authors of accepted papers are responsible to obtain a visa, if required, in a timely manner so as to meet the above expectations.

=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/registration/ ===
Link: REGISTRATION
Title: REGISTRATION - Smartcomp 2026
REGISTRATION - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Registration
Registration for IEEE SmartComp 2026 follows an
all-inclusive model
, providing access to the full conference program. Each registered participant will be entitled to:
Attendance at the main conference sessions
Participation in all workshops and co-located events
Access to coffee breaks and catering services
Admission to social events included in the conference program
Separate registrations for individual workshops are not available.
All attendees, including authors and non-authors, are required to register.
Given an accepted paper for any of the conference tracks (main, industry, workshop, poster/demo paper, or PhD forum), at least one of the authors listed on the paper must attend the conference in person and present the paper according to the schedule provided in advance.
Registration Categories
Participants may register under one of the following categories:
Full Registration (IEEE Member / Non-member)
Student Registration (IEEE Member / Non-member)
Discounted rates are available for IEEE members and students, subject to the conditions below. If you are not already an IEEE member, you may join
here
.
Author Registration Policy
In order for accepted contributions to be included in the conference program and proceedings, the following conditions apply:
Each accepted paper, whether in the main conference, workshops or affiliated events (except PhD Forum), requires a dedicated
full registration
A full registration covers
one paper
A full registration may also cover one additional contribution from satellite tracks (e.g., Demo, Posters, PhD Forum). It does not cover workshop papers.
For authors with multiple accepted papers:
Each additional paper (whether in the main conference or workshops) beyond the first must be registered separately
A
reduced additional paper fee
is available when multiple papers are associated with the same registered author
Student registrations:
Student registrations
cannot be used to cover regular papers
(main conference or workshops)
They are only applicable to student-oriented tracks (e.g., PhD Forum), unless accompanied by a full registration
At least one author per accepted paper must be registered.
Important Dates
(To be confirmed)
Registrations Open:
2nd May 2026
Author registration deadline:
15th May 2026
Early registration deadline:
1st June 2026
Registration Entitlements
A valid registration includes:
Access to all technical sessions and workshops
Access to the conference proceedings
Coffee breaks and scheduled catering services
Participation in social events included in the official program
Payment Policy
Registrations are considered valid only upon receipt of
full payment
Payments must be completed through the official registration system
Any bank charges or transaction fees shall be borne by the participant
Cancellation and Refund Policy
General Refund Policy
Requests submitted within the specified deadline are eligible for a
partial refund
, subject to an administrative fee (€50)
No refunds will be issued after the stated deadline
Visa-related Cases
In the event of visa denial, a refund may be granted upon submission of
official supporting documentation
Author Registrations
Each accepted paper must remain associated with a valid full registration
Failure to comply may result in removal of the paper from the program and proceedings
Additional charges may apply for:
Extra pages exceeding the allowed limit (€100 per page)
Additional tickets for social events (e.g., banquet) (€80)
Registration Fees
Early Registration
Category | Fee
Full – IEEE Member | €820
Full – Non-member | €975
Student – IEEE Member | €550
Student – Non-member | €660
Additional Paper Fee (same author) | €350
Late / On-site Registration
Category | Fee
Full – IEEE Member | €980
Full – Non-member | €1170
Student – IEEE Member | €660
Student – Non-member | €800
Additional Items
Item | Fee
Extra page | €100
Additional banquet ticket | €80
Notes on Additional Papers
The additional paper fee applies only to authors who have already completed a full registration.
Each additional paper must be associated with the same registered author in order to qualify for the reduced fee.
Final Remarks
These policies are aligned with standard IEEE conference practices and comparable international events.
The Organizing Committee reserves the right to make adjustments if necessary.
Participants are strongly encouraged to complete registration within the early deadline to benefit from reduced fees.
REGISTER HERE

bruteforce.context:
=== PAGE: https://smartcomp2026.unime.it/call-phd-forum/ ===
Title: CALL FOR PHD FORUM - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR PHD FORUM - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for PhD Forum
Call for Contributions: SmartComp 2026 PhD Forum
The Ph.D. Forum at SmartComp 2026 provides an excellent opportunity for Ph.D. students to present their work in progress to the scientific community and obtain individual feedback from leading researchers in the field. This forum is intended to provide doctoral students with a platform to interact with established researchers from both academia and industry and get useful guidance on the broader impacts of their research.
PhD Forum Format
The Forum will be organized as a workshop co-located with SmartComp. In addition, the Forum will be supplemented by a one-minute-madness session plus a poster session during the main conference, to facilitate extended interaction opportunities between Ph.D. student participants and researchers from academia, industry, and government.
During the workshop, senior members of the community will be present to provide constructive technical comments to each attendee. The Forum will provide a working environment for students to refine and practice their presentations, helping them improve communication skills, and to collaboratively develop research ideas for the PhD research and beyond. Special recognition will be given to the most innovative and/or most promising research project(s) presented at the Ph.D. Forum based on the pitch talk, the presented poster, and the discussion with the participants during the poster session.
Current Ph.D. students at any stage of their career are encouraged to submit a 3-page extended abstract about their research. Submissions are expected to include motivation of the importance and challenges to the particular problem, and initial results that support either the importance of the research problem or viability of solutions.
The abstract should include the author’s name, affiliation and email address. The name of the supervisor should be marked (“supervised by …”) on the paper, under the author’s name. Authors of accepted submissions are expected to participate in-person. The proceedings of the Forum will be included in the proceedings of the main conference.
Submission Guidelines
Submissions must be in PDF format, be no more than three pages in length (all included) and adhere to the IEEE two-column conference format (the same format used for paper submissions to SmartComp2026).
All manuscripts must be registered and submitted through the
EDAS Submission Site.
Participation
Authors of accepted submissions are expected to participate in the Ph.D. Forum (a half-day workshop immediately prior to the main conference), and to deliver an elevator pitch and A1 poster presentation for their work during the main program of the SmartComp2026 conference.
Participants who are current full-time students are eligible for the student registration fee. However, please note that any other publication in SmartComp—including main conference papers, posters, demos, workshop papers, or work-in-progress submissions—requires at least one author to register at the full rate. If a student planning to attend the PhD Forum is also a co-author of any such publication, and no other co-author is registering at the full rate, the student must register at the full rate and is not eligible for the student rate.
Some students will also be able to apply for travel grants (when details are available, they will be published on the SmartComp website).
Important Dates

15th April 2026
24th April 2026
Acceptance notification:
20th April 2026
3rd May 2026
Camera ready:
30th April 2026
6th May 2026
SMARTCOMP 2026 PhD Forum Chairs
Franca Delmastro
, IIT-CNR, Italy

, University of Kentucky, USA

IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
Messina, Italy - Department of Engineering
22 - 25 June 2026
PROGRAMME
REGISTRATION
IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain enabled by advances in sensor-based technologies, Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems, edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, ambient intelligence, and artificial intelligence.
Applications of smart computing can be found in diverse societal domains, including transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, industrial systems, precision farming, and entertainment. Algorithmic and systems advancements in edge, fog, or cloud computing, mobile and pervasive computing, sensor networking, and social computing are accelerating such innovation while expanding its influence in everyday ways of living.
SMARTCOMP 2026 welcomes novel, cutting-edge and creative research contributions. Authors should discuss the importance, timeliness, and significance of their work as well as limitations, implications, future opportunities and open research directions.

Link: CALLS
Title: CALL FOR PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR PAPERS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Research Papers
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
Smart computing is a multidisciplinary domain enabled by advances in sensor-based technologies, Internet of Things, cyber-physical systems, edge computing, big data analytics, machine learning, cognitive computing, ambient intelligence, and artificial intelligence.
Applications of smart computing can be found in diverse societal domains, including transportation, energy, environmental protection, smart and connected communities, healthcare, industrial systems, precision farming, and entertainment. Algorithmic and systems advancements in edge, fog, or cloud computing, mobile and pervasive computing, sensor networking, and social computing are accelerating such innovation while expanding its influence in everyday ways of living.
SMARTCOMP 2026 welcomes novel, cutting-edge and creative research contributions. Authors should discuss the importance, timeliness, and significance of their work as well as limitations, implications, future opportunities and open research directions.
Main Research Track
SMARTCOMP 2026 solicits papers that address the core challenges of smart computing, focusing on the design and development of scalable, dependable systems, and on leveraging computing technologies to enhance resource sustainability and enhance sustainable quality of human life.
Topics of interest include – but are not limited– to the following:
Emerging Smart Computing Paradigms
Algorithms for Smart Computing
AI and Machine Learning in Smart Computing
Mobile and Ubiquitous Smart Systems, Wearable Computing and IoT
Intelligent Cyber-Physical Systems
Cloud, Edge, and Fog based Smart Computing Platforms
Data Engineering and Analytics for Smart Computing
Distributed Sensing, AI, and Computation
Middleware for Smart Systems
Novel Communication Paradigms in Smart Computing
Low Power Communication and Computing for Smart Systems
Social Computing and Smart Systems
Quantum Computing in Smart Systems
Security, Privacy, and Trust in Smart Computing
Fairness and Socio-technical Aspects of Smart Computing
Interdisciplinary Approaches to Smart Computing
Novel applications of Smart Computing in emerging and high potential fields (e.g. Smart Communities, Health, Industry, Transport, Energy, Space, Agrifood, …)
Important Dates
Paper Submission:
1st February 2026
16th February (AoE)
Acceptance Notification:
15th April 2026
Camera Ready:
8th May 2026
Conference:
22 to 25 June 2026
All deadlines are Anywhere on Earth.
Submission Guidelines
Paper submissions must be no longer than 8 pages including references and formatted using the two-column IEEE proceedings template. All submitted papers following these requirements will be peer reviewed by the Technical Program Committee. All presented papers will be published in the conference proceedings and submitted to the IEEEXplore Digital Library.
The conference will be in-person and no virtual or remote presentations will be permitted.
Accepted papers must be presented in person by one of the authors under full registration. Failure to meet this requirement will result in the exclusion of the paper from the final proceedings, the conference program, and the IEEE Digital Library.
Authors of accepted papers are responsible to obtain an Italian visa, if required, in a timely manner so as to meet the above expectations.
The conference applies a single-blind review process.
Submit your paper here
.
Paper registration opens on November 1st, 2025
One additional page for the camera-ready version can be purchased after the paper review and selection.
BEST PAPER AWARD and Editorial Follow-Ups
The authors of the best paper will receive an award in recognition to their contribution to the field.
Extended versions of selected high-quality papers accepted to the main conference will be considered for publication in
Elsevier’s Pervasive and Mobile Computing journal (IF: 3.5)
Technical Program Committee Co-Chairs
Daniela Nicklas
George Roussos
Contact Information
For any questions about the conference, please contact the TPC Co-Chairs.

Call for Industry Track Papers
SMARTCOMP 2026, the 12th edition of the premier conference on smart computing, will be held in Messina, Italy from 22nd to 25th June 2026.
In addition to the main research track, the conference invites innovative applications and advances in the industrial use of smart computing technology.
Industry Track
The industry track will provide a forum to discuss novel ideas and emerging results, present innovative applications and tools, and bring about novel research questions, approaches, and directions with an industry focus. The conference industry track draws upon many industrial applications such as transportation, energy, environmental protection, resource management, healthcare, security, banking, entertainment, and social media.
An important criterion for papers to be submitted to the Industry Track is the clear industrial association, either in the form of an extensive evaluation of research results in industrial use cases or the active participation of industry representatives as co-authors.
Accepted papers will be published as part of a separate section of the main conference proceedings that highlights the Industry relevance of the submission.
Industry Track Topics include, but are not limited to, the following:

Link: CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS
Title: CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS - Smartcomp 2026
CALL FOR WORKSHOP PROPOSALS - Smartcomp 2026
Skip to content
Call for Workshop Proposals
The organising committee of the IEEE International Conference on Smart Computing (SMARTCOMP) 2026 invites proposals for workshops to be held in conjunction with the main conference in Messina, Italy, on June 22
nd
, 2026. IEEE SMARTCOMP is the premier forum for exploring cutting-edge research, innovation, and applications in the rapidly evolving domain of smart computing, encompassing advancements in sensor-based technologies, IoT, cyber-physical systems, edge and cloud computing, big data, machine learning, and AI.
The workshops at IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 aim to encourage researchers, practitioners, and industry experts to exchange ideas, discuss emerging trends, and explore novel approaches within the field of smart computing.
The Workshops co-chairs of IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 invite proposals for half or full day (22
nd
June 2026) workshops affiliated with the conference. Workshops should provide opportunities for deep dives into focused areas, promote lively interactions, and stimulate collaborative research in both emerging and established topics.
Submission Guidelines
Each workshop proposal must include the following:
Workshop Title and Acronym, where applicable.
Organisers (Workshops may have
up to three organisers
. It is strongly recommended that organisers belong to at least two different institutions):
Names
Affiliations
Short bios of up to 200 words for each organizer
A brief description (up to 2 pages) including:
Research and technological issues to be covered in the workshop;
Justification for organising the workshop in SMARTCOMP 2026;
A short discussion on the distinguishing features of this workshop compared to other available workshops and conferences;
A description of the research groups the workshop aims to attract;
Names of potential Program Committee members;
A tentative format of the workshop (e.g., keynotes, paper presentations) and duration (e.g., one-day, half-day), and a strategy to facilitate lively discussions and involvement of the attendees;
If applicable, a description of past versions of the workshop, including dates, organisers, number of submitted and accepted papers, and number of attendees;
An estimate of the expected number of submissions and likely attendees if this is the first time such a workshop proposal is submitted to IEEE SMARTCOMP.
A tentative Call for Papers (as complete as possible);
A description of the plans for publicity and workshop Website.
Submission Method
Workshop proposals should be submitted in PDF format by email with the subject line “IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 Workshop Proposal” to the Workshop Co-Chairs: Dirk Pesch (dirk.pesch@ucc.ie) and Francesca Righetti (francesca.righetti@unipi.it), no later than Wednesday, 10 December 2025. More details on IEEE SMARTCOMP 2026 can be found at our home page
here
.

Notification of Acceptance

nd
June 2026
Publication
Each accepted paper will require a full SMARTCOMP registration (workshops only registrations will not be available) and in-person presentation. All accepted papers presented in the workshop will be published in the proceedings of the conference and published in the IEEE Xplore Digital Library and Scopus indexed. Papers that are not presented at the workshop will not be published in the proceedings.
